assessor_raw:
## Baseline Assessment — eval_scenario_332, Run 2

The design compares a transformer + network/metadata features against a text-only logistic regression baseline. The network feature set includes 'reporting intensity' — the count of distinct recipients who flag a message through the security toolbar within 24 hours post-delivery. Two independent problems invalidate the inclusion of this feature: first, user-reported phishing is one of the phishing=1 label sources, creating a direct causal path from high reporting intensity to phishing=1 label assignment (target leakage); second, the feature is entirely post-delivery and unavailable at real-time inference time, making any evaluation metric that incorporates this feature unreproducible in production. The 24-hour window does not fix the availability problem — it merely restricts the observation window. The design correctly locks other features (file hash reputation, domain reputation) to training-period data, but fails to apply the same discipline to reporting intensity. Verdict: **critique_wins**
